global_context.scene_description: A magazine-style testimonial ad layout. The image is divided into a portrait and a quote area, designed like an editorial testimonial card. On the LEFT half: a head-and-shoulders portrait of a regular-guy Phoenix homeowner — Mike, white male, late 30s to early 40s, working-class, slight stubble, casual blue short-sleeve button-down shirt, trimmed brown hair, weathered tan from Phoenix sun, sitting on his front porch in late afternoon Phoenix golden light. He has a slight genuine smile — not posed, more like he's mid-conversation with the photographer. Behind him, the soft blur of his Phoenix neighborhood — gravel landscaping, saguaros, tan stucco. He looks exactly like a real customer would, NOT a stock model. On the RIGHT half: a clean off-white background with a large bold black serif quote: 'I PUT IT OFF FOR 2 YEARS. WHEN I FINALLY CALLED, THEY MADE IT PAINLESS.' Below the quote, smaller text in dark grey: '— Mike R., Chandler'. Below that, a row of five gold stars. The quote text is the focal element — large, breaking across multiple lines, designed like a real review/testimonial layout. Lighting on the portrait is warm golden hour, soft, flattering, real. The whole composition feels like a Consumer Reports review or a polished local-business testimonial — trustworthy, real, not over-glossed. Cinematic but documentary-real, NOT staged-stock-photo.
